seoMetaTitle: Bronze Tech Solutions | Industrial Automation, EV Charging, Renewable Energy | BeiLuo
seoMetaDescription: Explore Bronze Tech application solutions for industrial automation, electric vehicle charging, renewable energy systems, and medical devices. Complete BOM and technical specifications.

faqs.question: What application solutions does Bronze Tech offer?
faqs.answer: Bronze Tech offers comprehensive interconnect solutions for multiple industries: 1) Industrial Automation - Complete connectivity solutions for factory automation including M12/M8 circular connectors for sensors and actuators, board-to-board connectors for PLC modules, and wire-to-board connectors for control panels. 2) EV Charging Infrastructure - High-voltage, high-current connectors for charging stations and onboard systems up to 1000V DC. 3) Renewable Energy - Robust connectors for solar inverters, wind turbines, and energy storage systems with 25+ year service life. Each solution includes optimized BOM recommendations, technical specifications, and FAE engineering support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore each solution page to find detailed BOM lists, technical specs, and customer success stories for your application.

faqs.question: How do I choose the right Bronze Tech solution for my project?
faqs.answer: Choosing the right solution depends on your application requirements: 1) For factory automation with sensors, actuators, and industrial Ethernet, select the Industrial Automation solution with M12 and M8 circular connectors. 2) For electric vehicle charging infrastructure or high-voltage power electronics, choose the EV Charging solution with high-voltage board-to-board connectors. 3) For solar inverters, wind power, or energy storage, select the Renewable Energy solution with outdoor-rated connectors. Consider factors like voltage requirements, environmental conditions, protocol compatibility, and certification needs. Our FAE team can provide personalized recommendations based on your specific project parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your project requirements for personalized solution recommendations and BOM optimization.

faqs.question: What is included in a Bronze Tech solution?
faqs.answer: Each Bronze Tech solution includes: 1) Complete BOM List - Curated list of connectors optimized for the application with quantities and descriptions. 2) Technical Specifications - Operating temperature, protection ratings, vibration/shock resistance, and EMI shielding specifications. 3) Core Advantages - Key benefits of the solution including environmental reliability, protocol compatibility, and installation efficiency. 4) Customer Cases - Real-world success stories with quantified results. 5) FAE Insights - Expert engineering recommendations and best practices. 6) Solution-Specific FAQs - Detailed answers to common application questions. This comprehensive approach ensures you have all the information needed for successful implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete solution package including BOM, technical specs, and FAE insights before starting your design.

faqs.question: Can Bronze Tech provide custom solutions for unique applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bronze Tech specializes in custom interconnect solutions for applications that cannot be met with standard products. Our custom capabilities include: 1) Modified standard products - Custom pin counts, special plating, or unique form factors. 2) Application-specific designs - Connectors designed specifically for your mechanical and electrical requirements. 3) Integrated cable assemblies - Overmolded cable assemblies with custom lengths and configurations. 4) High-voltage designs - Custom connectors for applications up to 1500V DC with enhanced creepage and clearance. The custom design process typically takes 8-12 weeks from requirements definition to production. Our engineering team works closely with customers throughout the development process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our custom solutions team with your specific requirements for a design consultation and proposal.

faqs.question: What industries does Bronze Tech serve?
faqs.answer: Bronze Tech serves a wide range of industries with specialized interconnect solutions: 1) Industrial Automation - Factory automation, process control, robotics, and machine vision systems. 2) Automotive - EV charging infrastructure, battery management systems, and onboard electronics. 3) Renewable Energy - Solar inverters, wind power systems, and energy storage. 4) Medical Devices - Diagnostic equipment and patient monitoring systems. 5) Telecommunications - Base stations and network infrastructure. 6) Aerospace and Defense - Ruggedized connectors for harsh environments. Each industry has specific requirements for reliability, certification, and environmental resistance that our solutions address.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select your industry from our solutions page to find application-specific connector recommendations.

solutions.name: Industrial Automation Interconnect Solutions
solutions.description: Comprehensive interconnect solutions for industrial automation applications including PLCs, sensors, actuators, and control systems. Our M12 and M8 circular connectors, combined with industrial Ethernet solutions, provide reliable connectivity in harsh factory environments.
solutions.longDescription: Bronze Tech industrial automation solutions provide the connectivity backbone for modern manufacturing systems. Our solution includes M12 circular connectors for sensors and actuators, M8 connectors for compact devices, board-to-board connectors for PLC modules, and wire-to-board connectors for control panels. All components are rated for industrial environments with IP67 protection, vibration resistance, and EMI shielding. The solution supports major industrial protocols including Profinet, EtherCAT, and Modbus TCP. We provide complete BOM optimization, ensuring compatibility and reducing procurement complexity.

coreAdvantages.title: Harsh Environment Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: IP67-rated connectors withstand dust, moisture, and washdown environments typical in industrial settings. All components tested to industrial vibration and shock standards.

coreAdvantages.title: Protocol Compatibility
coreAdvantages.description: M12 connectors support major industrial Ethernet protocols including Profinet, EtherCAT, and EtherNet/IP with proper coding and shielding.

coreAdvantages.title: Complete Solution
coreAdvantages.description: Single-source BOM including circular, board-to-board, and wire-to-board connectors reduces procurement complexity and ensures compatibility.

coreAdvantages.title: Quick Installation
coreAdvantages.description: Field-attachable connectors and IDC options enable fast installation and maintenance, reducing downtime in production environments.

bomList.description: M12 8-pin straight connector for industrial Ethernet

keyComponents.description: M12 8-pin straight connector for industrial Ethernet

customerCases.challenge: Needed reliable connectivity for new automated welding line with 200+ sensors and actuators in high-vibration environment with frequent washdown requirements.
customerCases.solution: Implemented Bronze Tech M12/M8 circular connectors for field devices and board-to-board connectors for PLC modules. Standardized on IP67-rated connectors with metal housings for EMI protection.
customerCases.results: Zero connector-related downtime in first 12 months. Reduced installation time by 40% with standardized connectors. Passed automotive industry vibration testing.

customerCases.challenge: Required washdown-resistant connectors for food packaging equipment with daily high-pressure cleaning and chemical exposure.
customerCases.solution: Deployed IP67-rated M12 connectors with FKM seals and stainless steel coupling nuts. Used overmolded cable assemblies for maximum sealing reliability.
customerCases.results: Passed IP69K washdown testing. Zero moisture ingress after 6 months of daily cleaning. Reduced maintenance time by 30%.

faeInsights.insight: For industrial automation, the key is selecting connectors that can handle the environmental stress while maintaining signal integrity. I always recommend M12 connectors for any Ethernet-based automation system - they're the industry standard for good reason. The D-code is essential for industrial Ethernet (Profinet, EtherCAT), while A-code works for sensors and power. For high-vibration environments like welding lines, always use connectors with locking threads and specify metal housings for EMI shielding. One critical tip: always verify the coding before ordering - using the wrong coding can delay projects significantly.
faeInsights.logic: The selection process starts with protocol requirements - Ethernet needs D-code M12, sensors use A-code. Then consider environmental factors - IP67 for most industrial, IP68 for submersible. For high-vibration applications, locking mechanisms are essential. Finally, specify metal housings for any EMI-sensitive applications.
keyTakeaways: Use D-coded M12 for industrial Ethernet networks
Specify metal housings for EMI-sensitive applications
Include locking threads for high-vibration environments
Maintain proper torque (0.4-0.6 Nm) for IP rating integrity
Use dust caps when connectors are unmated
commonPitfalls: Ordering wrong coding (A-code vs D-code) for Ethernet applications
Ignoring EMI shielding requirements in noisy environments
Insufficient torque leading to IP rating failure
bestPractices: Standardize on M12 for Ethernet, M8 for compact sensors
Include 20% spare connectors in BOM for maintenance
Use right-angle connectors for space-constrained installations
Verify shield grounding for reliable data transmission

faqs.question: Which connector series should I use for industrial Ethernet?
faqs.answer: For industrial Ethernet (Profinet, EtherCAT, EtherNet/IP), use the CIR-M12 series with D-coding. The 8-pin configuration supports 100 Mbps Ethernet with proper shielding for EMI protection. Use panel mount receptacles on devices and straight or right-angle connectors on cables. Ensure proper grounding of the shield for best performance in electrically noisy environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use M12 D-code 8-pin for industrial Ethernet. Ensure proper shield grounding.

faqs.question: How do I protect connectors in washdown environments?
faqs.answer: For washdown environments, select IP67 or IP68-rated connectors like our CIR-M12 and CIR-M8 series. Ensure proper torque (0.4-0.6 Nm for M12) when mating to maintain seal integrity. Use dust caps when connectors are unmated. For extreme chemical exposure, specify the optional FKM (Viton) seals instead of standard NBR. Consider using cable glands with the same IP rating as the connectors to maintain system protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IP67/IP68 connectors. Maintain proper torque. Use dust caps when unmated.

solutions.name: Electric Vehicle Charging Solutions
solutions.description: High-current interconnect solutions for EV charging stations, onboard chargers, and battery management systems. Our power connectors support up to 50A per contact with robust designs for outdoor and automotive environments.
solutions.longDescription: Bronze Tech EV charging solutions address the unique challenges of electric vehicle infrastructure. Our high-current board-to-board connectors handle power distribution in charging stations, while custom cable assemblies provide reliable connections between power modules and charging cables. All components are designed for outdoor exposure with UV resistance, wide temperature ranges, and high-current capacity. The solution includes connectors for AC input, DC output, control pilot signals, and communication interfaces. Components meet automotive and industrial standards for safety and reliability.
features: High voltage rating up to 1000V DC

coreAdvantages.title: High Current Capacity
coreAdvantages.description: Power connectors rated up to 50A per contact with low contact resistance, minimizing power loss and heat generation in charging systems.

coreAdvantages.title: High Voltage Safety
coreAdvantages.description: Custom high-voltage connectors with enhanced creepage and clearance distances for safe operation up to 1000V DC.

coreAdvantages.title: Outdoor Durability
coreAdvantages.description: UV-resistant materials and wide temperature range (-40°C to +125°C) ensure reliable operation in outdoor charging environments.

coreAdvantages.title: Automotive Qualification
coreAdvantages.description: Components available with AEC-Q200 qualification and testing to automotive standards for onboard applications.

bomList.description: Custom overmolded cable assembly for control signals

customerCases.challenge: Needed reliable high-power interconnects for 150kW DC fast charging stations with outdoor exposure, high vibration, and thermal management requirements.
customerCases.solution: Implemented Bronze Tech high-current board-to-board connectors and custom high-voltage interconnects with enhanced creepage distances for 1000V operation.
customerCases.results: Successfully deployed 500+ charging stations. Zero thermal issues with 50A rated connectors. Passed UL certification for EV charging equipment.

customerCases.challenge: Required compact, high-voltage connectors for onboard charger with limited space and strict automotive qualification requirements.
customerCases.solution: Used custom high-voltage board-to-board connectors with 8mm creepage and automotive-grade materials. Optimized PCB layout for thermal management.
customerCases.results: Passed AEC-Q200 qualification. Achieved 40% space reduction vs previous design. Operating reliably in 10,000+ vehicles.

faeInsights.insight: EV charging applications demand connectors that can handle high current without excessive heating. The key is proper contact sizing and ensuring adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation. For high-voltage DC applications, creepage and clearance are critical - standard connectors may not provide adequate spacing for 1000V operation. I always recommend thermal testing under actual operating conditions. For outdoor installations, UV resistance is often overlooked but critical for long-term reliability. The CUS-BTB-HV series was specifically developed to address these high-voltage safety requirements.
faeInsights.logic: Start with voltage requirements - 400V systems need different spacing than 1000V systems. Then calculate current requirements and verify temperature rise. For outdoor installations, specify UV-resistant materials. Finally, ensure automotive qualification if required for onboard applications.
keyTakeaways: Use 5.08mm pitch or larger for high-current applications
Verify temperature rise under actual operating conditions
Ensure adequate creepage distance for DC voltage levels
Specify UV-resistant materials for outdoor installations
Consider AEC-Q200 qualification for onboard applications
commonPitfalls: Insufficient creepage distance for high-voltage DC
Ignoring UV degradation in outdoor installations
bestPractices: Use 2oz copper minimum for high-current PCB traces
Implement HVIL (High Voltage Interlock Loop) circuits
Add thermal monitoring for critical connections

faqs.question: What connectors are recommended for EV charging power modules?
faqs.answer: For EV charging power modules, we recommend the BTB-50 series (5.08mm pitch) for high-current applications up to 15A per contact, and the CUS-BTB-HV series for high-voltage DC bus connections up to 1000V. The 5.08mm pitch provides adequate spacing and current capacity for power distribution. For control signals, use the BTB-27 series (1.27mm pitch). Always verify temperature rise under actual operating conditions and ensure adequate PCB copper area (2oz minimum recommended) for heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BTB-50 for power, CUS-BTB-HV for high voltage, BTB-27 for signals.

faqs.question: How do I ensure safety in high-voltage EV applications?
faqs.answer: For high-voltage EV applications (400V-1000V), safety is paramount. Use connectors specifically designed for high voltage like our CUS-BTB-HV series with enhanced creepage (8mm) and clearance (6mm). Ensure connectors are rated for your maximum working voltage with safety margin. Use touch-safe designs where user access is possible. Implement proper interlocking and HVIL (High Voltage Interlock Loop) circuits. All high-voltage connectors should be clearly marked and accessible only to qualified personnel. Follow applicable standards including IEC 60664 for insulation coordination.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-voltage rated connectors. Implement HVIL. Follow IEC 60664.
